ranges.label: Normal
ranges.description: Your child's total difficulties score falls within the normal range, indicating that emotional, behavioral, and social functioning are generally age-appropriate with no clinically significant problems across assessed domains.
suggestions: Continue fostering a warm, supportive home environment with open communication about feelings and daily experiences
Maintain regular routines and age-appropriate expectations, as consistency supports healthy emotional and behavioral development
Stay engaged with your child's school and social activities to monitor ongoing well-being and reinforce positive behaviors

ranges.label: Borderline
ranges.description: Your child's total difficulties score is at a borderline level, suggesting emerging concerns that may warrant closer attention. Some emotional, behavioral, or social difficulties are present and should be monitored over time.
suggestions: Monitor your child's emotional and behavioral patterns more closely over the coming weeks, noting any specific situations that trigger difficulties
Maintain open and regular communication with teachers and school staff to gain a broader perspective on your child's functioning across settings
Consider completing the SDQ again in three to six months to track whether difficulties are improving, stable, or worsening over time
If concerns persist or increase, consult a child psychologist or pediatrician who can conduct a more comprehensive developmental assessment

ranges.label: Abnormal
ranges.description: Your child's total difficulties score is in the abnormal range, indicating clinically significant emotional, behavioral, or social problems that likely affect daily functioning at home, school, or in peer relationships.
suggestions: Seek a professional evaluation from a child and adolescent mental health specialist who can identify specific problem areas and recommend targeted interventions
Work collaboratively with your child's school to develop supportive strategies, as difficulties at this level often affect academic performance and peer interactions
Focus on understanding which specific domains are most affected by reviewing subscale patterns, as this guides the most appropriate type of professional support
Early professional intervention for children with elevated scores has strong evidence for improving long-term emotional, behavioral, and social outcomes
Ensure your child feels supported rather than blamed, as children with high difficulties scores benefit most from empathetic and patient caregiving approaches

ranges.label: Low Well-being
ranges.description: Your overall PERMA well-being score is low, suggesting that multiple dimensions of flourishing need attention. You may be experiencing limited positive emotions, weak social connections, low engagement, or unclear sense of purpose.
suggestions: Start with small, achievable daily habits that promote well-being, such as writing down three things you are grateful for each evening before bed
Seek meaningful social connections by reaching out to trusted friends or family members, as positive relationships are one of the strongest predictors of well-being
Identify one activity that genuinely interests you and commit to engaging in it regularly, as even brief periods of flow can significantly boost overall well-being
Consider consulting a mental health professional or well-being coach who can help you develop a personalized plan for improving your psychological functioning
Be patient with yourself, as building well-being is a gradual process and even modest improvements in one PERMA dimension often create positive spillover effects

ranges.label: Moderate Well-being
ranges.description: Your well-being is at a moderate level, indicating a reasonable degree of flourishing with identifiable areas for growth. Some PERMA dimensions may be stronger than others, and targeted effort can help you achieve greater balance.
suggestions: Review your subscale scores to identify which specific PERMA dimensions are lowest, and focus your improvement efforts on those particular areas first
Strengthen your sense of meaning by reflecting on your core values and aligning more of your daily activities with what you find genuinely important and purposeful
Invest in deepening your existing relationships through regular quality time, active listening, and expressing appreciation for the people who matter most to you
Set personally meaningful goals and track your progress toward them, as a sense of accomplishment contributes substantially to overall psychological well-being

ranges.label: High Well-being
ranges.description: Your overall PERMA well-being score is high, reflecting strong flourishing across multiple dimensions of positive psychological functioning including positive emotions, meaningful engagement, supportive relationships, and clear purpose.
suggestions: Continue maintaining the habits and relationships that support your high well-being, and remain mindful of any changes that could disrupt this positive balance
Consider using your strengths to help others flourish, as mentoring, volunteering, and supporting others' growth can further enhance your own sense of meaning and connection
Periodically reassess your well-being to ensure sustained balance, as life transitions and new challenges can shift your PERMA profile over time
